title: Inside the Senate G.O.P. Meltdown Over Trump’s Fund
summary: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, went to Capitol Hill to allay Republicans’ concerns over a fund to pay people who claim government mistreatment. It did not go well.

title: Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind
summary: Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides.

title: Republicans Delay Budget Votes as They Balk at Trump’s Fund
summary: The G.O.P. abandoned its plan to quickly pass a $72 billion immigration crackdown bill as senators revolted over his plan to pay people who claim government persecution.

title: Senate Republicans Revolt Over Trump’s Fund
summary: Also, Democrats release a draft report on what they did wrong in 2024. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: D.N.C. 2024 Election Autopsy Reopens Wounds of Harris Loss
summary: The report partly blamed aides to Joseph R. Biden Jr. and Kamala Harris for her defeat. But it was widely seen as a poorly executed examination, bringing fresh scrutiny to Democrats.

title: 5 Takeaways From the Democrats’ Autopsy of Kamala Harris’s 2024 Loss
summary: A draft report released by the Democratic National Committee argued that Ms. Harris did not sufficiently separate herself from President Joseph R. Biden Jr.

title: Cuba Celebrates Raúl Castro After the U.S. Accuses Him of Murder
summary: Cuban officials closed ranks around Mr. Castro, their embattled former president, who was indicted on murder charges in the downing of two civilian planes 30 years ago.

title: Cuban Pilot in Florida Is a Defendant in Raúl Castro Indictment
summary: The pilot, Luis González-Pardo, was one of the defendants in the indictment that included former Cuban President Raúl Castro.

title: Outside Colbert’s Final Show, Fans From Near and Far Line Up for a Farewell
summary: Hundreds of people, including a pope, staked out the Ed Sullivan Theater in Manhattan, hoping to get in, or just get a glimpse.

title: Stephen Colbert’s Final ‘Late Show’ Marks the End of an Era
summary: Stephen Colbert’s “Colbert Report” satirized politics. Then his “Late Show” confronted a moment when politics became self-satirizing.

title: Experimental Drug Yields Dramatic Weight Loss
summary: People who got the injection, retatrutide, lost 28 percent of their body weight on average after 80 weeks, Eli Lilly said.

title: Trump Officials’ Strict Stand on Ebola Leaves Health Experts ‘Stunned’
summary: Public health experts say the administration’s quarantine orders go beyond what is needed to prevent the U.S. spread of Ebola and hantavirus.

title: He Sued the N.Y.P.D. He Advised ‘Homeland.’ Now He’s Mamdani’s Lawyer.
summary: Ramzi Kassem is accustomed to challenging authority. As the chief counsel to Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City, he finds himself on the other side.

title: A Powerful El Niño Is Forming. If History Is a Guide, It Could Hit Hard.
summary: The biggest episodes of the past have altered the course of human events, according to researchers. An emerging one is drawing historic comparisons.

title: NOAA’s 2026 Atlantic Hurricane Forecast Sees Fewer Storms, but There’s a Catch
summary: El Niño may keep the Atlantic quieter, but it could bring more storms to the Pacific.

title: Elim Chan Becomes First Female Music Director of San Francisco Symphony
summary: Elim Chan has been named the symphony’s next music director. She joins a small field of female conductors at American orchestras, as San Francisco rebuilds after Esa-Pekka Salonen.

title: Publishing’s Latest Piracy Problem: Audiobooks on YouTube
summary: Illegal, synthetically narrated copies of “The Hunger Games,” hit self-help books and everything in between are increasingly common on the platform.

title: Audible Opens a Bookless Bookstore
summary: Booksellers are trying to figure out how to market audiobooks in person. At a new pop-up, the Amazon-owned company is betting on listening rooms, branded merch and “story tenders.”

title: Ebola Is a Terrible Disease. I Saw It Firsthand.
summary: The Trump administration cut the programs meant to coordinate an Ebola response.

title: Indicting Castro Isn’t Justice
summary: How can Cubans move forward if they aren’t ready to reckon with their past?

title: Trump Says He Will ‘Try and Make’ Son Don Jr.’s Wedding, but Timing ‘Not Good’
summary: President Trump said the war with Iran and “other things” would make it difficult for him to make the wedding this weekend.

title: A Very Lonely Caterpillar, Possibly the Last of Its Kind, Has Died
summary: The Sacramento Mountains checkerspot butterfly hasn’t been seen in the wild since 2022. The caterpillar was the last individual in human care.

title: Alberta Separation: What to Know Ahead of Oct. 19 Vote on Staying in Canada
summary: Premier Danielle Smith announced that she would ask citizens to vote on whether they want to stay in Canada, or hold a referendum to secede.
